verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with the recommendation; A: "start with a plain in-memory cache", B: "default to a plain in-memory cache".
verdict.decisiveness: B is cleaner: "in-memory unless..."; A weakens the single prescription with more branches and a follow-up question.
verdict.fabrication: Both use unverified latency numbers; A: "~100–1000× slower", B: "~0.1–1ms".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it names concrete libraries: "Caffeine", "lru-cache", "cachetools", "MemoryCache".
